memory-system-v2-architecture.value: Memory system v2.0 uses a dual-layer architecture: curated memories in .ai-state/memory.json (JSON, schema v2.0 with type ontology, provenance, soft delete) and automatic observations in .ai-state/observations.jsonl (append-only JSONL captured by hooks with zero LLM cost). The inject_memory.py hook injects curated context into every subagent via SubagentStart additionalContext (requires hookEventName field in output). JSON storage with Markdown presentation — the LLM never reads raw JSON.
memory-system-v2-architecture.summary: Memory v2.0: dual-layer (curated JSON + observation JSONL), hook-injected, zero LLM cost

memory-enforcement-architecture.value: Memory creation is enforced by three layers: (1) memory_gate.py Stop hook blocks main agent if >3 edits and 0 remember() calls, (2) validate_memory.py SubagentStop hook blocks subagents if >5 edits and 0 remember() calls (exempt: Explore, sentinel, doc-engineer, Plan), (3) inject_memory.py appends a memory obligation footer to every subagent's context. The memory-protocol rule uses mandatory language. This replaced the original design where remember() calls were purely voluntary — which resulted in 0 organic memories across 300+ observations.
memory-enforcement-architecture.summary: Three-layer memory enforcement: Stop gate, SubagentStop gate, inject obligation footer

ecosystem-health-2026-04-04.value: Full system verification 2026-04-05: Grade A (4th consecutive). 536/536 tests pass (171 task-chronograph, 365 memory-mcp). Token budget improved to 84.3% (12,652/15,000) from 91.8% — 15.7% headroom now. 77 artifacts (29 skills, 12 agents, 8 rules, 15 commands, 18 hooks, 13 ADRs, 2 MCP servers). 0 critical/important findings, 6 suggested. Hygiene items: stale symlink ~/.claude/rules/swe/decision-tracking.md -> deleted file; 6 git stashes; 2 empty local branches; hook-crafting orphaned exit-code-semantics.md ref. All cosmetic.
ecosystem-health-2026-04-04.summary: System verification 2026-04-05: Grade A, 536/536 tests pass, token budget improved to 84.3%, hygiene items only

hook-output-requires-hookEventName.value: Claude Code hooks that output additionalContext MUST include hookEventName in the hookSpecificOutput JSON. Without it, the additionalContext is silently ignored and never injected into the agent context. Format: {"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "SubagentStart", "additionalContext": "..."}}
hook-output-requires-hookEventName.summary: Hook additionalContext requires hookEventName field or injection is silently ignored

stop-hook-gate-pattern.value: The Stop hook gate pattern enforces pre-completion checklists: a sync Stop hook scans the transcript, exits 2 with {"decision": "block", "reason": "..."} on stderr when criteria aren't met, and checks stop_hook_active to prevent infinite loops (exit 0 on second invocation). This pattern is reusable for any "before you stop" enforcement — test runs, commit checks, memory persistence. The key design: first invocation blocks with actionable feedback, second invocation always passes.
stop-hook-gate-pattern.summary: Stop hook gate pattern: sync hook + exit 2 + stop_hook_active check for pre-completion enforcement

hook-additionalContext-supported-events.value: additionalContext in hook output is only supported for specific events: PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, SubagentStart, Notification, PreToolUse (exit 0 only). Stop and SubagentStop do NOT support additionalContext — output is silently ignored. For those events, use decision: "block" + exit 2 + stderr JSON instead. This caused validate_memory.py to be dead code for months — it generated warnings that nobody ever saw.
hook-additionalContext-supported-events.summary: additionalContext only works on 7 specific hook events; Stop/SubagentStop must use exit 2 + stderr

async-hooks-cannot-deliver-feedback.value: Async hooks (async: true) cannot reliably deliver additionalContext to Claude — the agent may move on before the hook output arrives. Any hook that needs Claude to see and act on its output MUST be sync (async: false). This was the root cause of validate_memory.py being dead code: it was registered as async at SubagentStop, so its warnings were generated but never reached the parent agent. Always verify async vs sync when a hook's purpose is to influence agent behavior.
async-hooks-cannot-deliver-feedback.summary: Async hooks silently drop feedback — any hook influencing agent behavior must be sync

user-expects-memory-from-all-sessions.value: User expects that every meaningful session — not just code-editing ones — produces memories. Running a sentinel audit and only presenting results without storing insights was flagged as a gap. The principle: if a session produces findings, trends, or cross-session insights, the main agent must proactively call remember() regardless of whether the enforcement hooks would trigger. Memory creation is a responsibility, not just a compliance check.
user-expects-memory-from-all-sessions.summary: User expects memory creation from ALL meaningful sessions, not just code-editing ones

memory-gate-blind-spot-analysis-sessions.value: The memory gate enforcement (memory_gate.py for main agent, validate_memory.py for subagents) only triggers based on code edit counts (>=3 edits for main, >=5 for subagents). Analysis-heavy sessions — sentinel audits, research, reviews, planning — produce zero code edits and slip through with no memories stored. Additionally, sentinel/Explore/doc-engineer/Plan agents are explicitly exempt from validate_memory.py. This means the most insight-rich sessions (audits that reveal ecosystem health trends, research that surfaces architectural patterns) are the least likely to produce memories. The main agent must self-enforce memory creation after consuming analysis results, regardless of edit count.
memory-gate-blind-spot-analysis-sessions.summary: Memory gate only fires on code edits — analysis/audit sessions produce zero memories unless self-enforced

hook-duplication-scan-transcript.value: The _scan_transcript() function is duplicated across memory_gate.py and validate_memory.py hooks in .claude-plugin/hooks/. Both implement identical logic to scan Claude Code transcripts and classify tool events. This should be refactored into a shared utility module (already partially done with _hook_utils.py) to reduce maintenance burden and prevent divergence.
hook-duplication-scan-transcript.summary: _scan_transcript() duplicated in memory_gate.py and validate_memory.py — refactoring candidate for DRY

proactive-memory-vs-enforcement.value: Hooks alone cannot fully solve the memory gap. The fundamental issue is behavioral: the main agent must proactively call remember() during work, not wait for enforcement hooks to remind it. Hooks are a safety net, not a substitute for proactive memory. The Claude Code auto-memory system works because the instructions tell Claude to save memories as it discovers insights — it's instruction-driven, not enforcement-driven. Our system relies too heavily on enforcement (memory_gate at Stop, validate_memory at SubagentStop, remind_memory at commit) and not enough on the agent's own initiative to remember. The fix is both: enforce at boundaries AND instruct the agent to remember proactively.
proactive-memory-vs-enforcement.summary: Memory needs proactive agent behavior, not just enforcement hooks — hooks are safety nets, not substitutes

memory-enforcement-gap-commit-hook.value: Memory enforcement has a timing gap: SubagentStop hooks catch subagent work, Stop hook catches at session end, but nothing enforces memory for the main agent's mid-session work. The commit is the natural "done with work phase" checkpoint. Solution: remind_memory.py hook gated by commit_gate.sh that scans the transcript at commit time and warns if remember() was never called. Non-blocking (exit 0) — the Stop hook remains the hard gate. This still misses the case where the main agent is told the results by the user and then acts without any explicit remember() — the behavioral gap is that the agent itself should proactively remember, not just be reminded by hooks.
memory-enforcement-gap-commit-hook.summary: Memory enforcement gap: no mid-session check for main agent work; commit-time hook closes it partially

inject-memory-session-start-gap.value: inject_memory.py was only registered at SubagentStart, so the main agent never received curated memory or decision context at session start. This meant the main agent started every session blind — no memory priming, no obligation footer, no decisions context. Fixed by registering inject_memory.py at SessionStart too, with event detection (payload has agent_type for SubagentStart, not for SessionStart) to set the correct hookEventName. This mirrors how Claude Code's auto-memory works: MEMORY.md is always loaded at session start, creating a virtuous cycle of seeing memories → being primed to create more.
inject-memory-session-start-gap.summary: inject_memory.py was missing from SessionStart — main agent never got memory context; now fixed

agent-background-flag-mismatch.value: 8 of 11 Bg Safe agents (per swe-agent-coordination-protocol.md) are missing background: true in their agent frontmatter. Only cicd-engineer, doc-engineer, and sentinel have it set. Missing from: context-engineer, implementation-planner, implementer, researcher, skill-genesis, systems-architect, test-engineer, verifier. The background flag enables Claude Code to run agents in background mode; without it the coordination protocol Bg Safe designation has no runtime effect.
agent-background-flag-mismatch.summary: 8/11 Bg Safe agents missing background:true in frontmatter -- protocol vs runtime mismatch
